actual_cost.what: Cost after compression — tokens actually sent to the provider × price.
actual_cost.why: Real cost figure; what you are billed for
actual_cost.not_this: Not baseline cost — actual reflects compressed token count

baseline_cost.what: Cost without compression — what you would have paid at full token count.
baseline_cost.why: Reference point for measuring compression value
baseline_cost.not_this: Not actual spend — this is the counterfactual uncompressed cost

cache_tokens.what: Tokens served from provider cache rather than re-processed.
cache_tokens.why: Cache hits reduce cost and latency; indicates efficient prompt structure
cache_tokens.how: Reported in provider API response as cache_read_input_tokens
cache_tokens.not_this: Not raw or final tokens — cache tokens are a subset of input tokens

compression_ratio.what: How much smaller the compressed context is vs original (raw ÷ final).
compression_ratio.not_this: Not savings % — ratio is token count, savings % is cost-based

cost_per_request.what: Average cost per API call in the selected period.
cost_per_request.why: Normalizes cost for workload comparison regardless of volume
cost_per_request.not_this: Not baseline cost per request — uses actual (compressed) cost

error_rate.what: Fraction of requests that resulted in an API error.
error_rate.why: Key reliability metric; high error rates indicate provider or config issues
error_rate.not_this: Not retry rate — errors are final failures, retries may recover

estimated.what: Calculated from token counts × model pricing — not yet billed.
estimated.why: Default mode before billing reconciliation is configured
estimated.not_this: Not reconciled — may differ from actual bill due to pricing changes

final_tokens.what: Tokens actually sent to the provider after compression.
final_tokens.why: Determines actual cost; what the provider bills you for
final_tokens.how: raw_tokens minus tokens removed/collapsed by compression pipeline
final_tokens.not_this: Not raw tokens — final is after compression; always ≤ raw

latency_avg.what: Mean end-to-end request duration in milliseconds.
latency_avg.why: Baseline performance metric; average experience across all requests
latency_avg.not_this: Not p95/p99 — average masks outliers; use percentiles for tail latency

latency_p95.what: 95% of requests completed faster than this value (ms).
latency_p95.why: Captures tail latency; 1-in-20 requests were slower
latency_p95.not_this: Not average — p95 reveals slow outliers hidden by the mean

latency_p99.what: 99% of requests completed faster than this value (ms).
latency_p99.why: Captures extreme tail latency; 1-in-100 requests were slower
latency_p99.not_this: Not p95 — p99 is stricter; worst-case user experience

raw_tokens.what: Token count before any compression — full uncompressed context size.
raw_tokens.why: Starting point for measuring compression effect
raw_tokens.how: Counted by tokenizer on the original prompt before pipeline
raw_tokens.not_this: Not final tokens — raw is pre-compression; final is post-compression

reconciled.what: Data verified against actual provider billing records.
reconciled.why: Highest trust level — confirms estimated costs match real charges
reconciled.how: Billing API response cross-checked against TokenPak event log
reconciled.not_this: Not estimated — reconciled data requires configured billing API

request_count.what: Total API requests recorded in the selected time window.
request_count.why: Volume denominator used in rates, averages, and cost-per-request
request_count.how: COUNT of tp_events in period WHERE status IN (success, retry)
request_count.not_this: Not unique sessions — one session may generate many requests

retry_rate.what: Fraction of requests that required at least one retry.
retry_rate.why: Indicates transient failure rate; high retries increase latency and cost
retry_rate.not_this: Not error rate — retried requests may ultimately succeed

savings.what: Money saved through compression — baseline minus actual cost.
savings.not_this: Not cumulative lifetime savings — scoped to the selected date window

savings_pct.what: Savings as a percentage of baseline — how efficient compression is.
savings_pct.why: Normalizes savings across different spend levels for comparison
savings_pct.not_this: Not token reduction % — measures cost reduction, not token count reduction
